text: The surgeon begins the thymectomy by dissecting between the fat and the pericardium, using fenestrated forceps and a bipolar Maryland dissector to retract and dissect along the plane. The wristed articulation of the instruments allows precise orientation. The innominate vein, rhema vein, and superior vena cava (SVC) are visible, with subtle sweeping motions facilitating the dissection of fat between the innominate and rhema veins.
